privacy.redaction_note: Cloudflare tunnel URLs, dashboard token_REDACTED_FIELD_NAMEs, hook token_REDACTED_FIELD_NAMEs, bridge token_REDACTED_FIELD_NAMEs, and OAuth credentials are intentionally redacted.

source_evidence: OpenClaw agents_list: only main is currently exposed for subagent spawning.
OpenClaw sessions_list: one running webchat session for main.
OpenClaw cron list: no configured jobs.
OpenClaw nodes status: no paired nodes currently listed.
OpenClaw config lists agents main, home-windows-codex, and codex-memory-operator; hooks allow home-windows-codex and codex-memory-operator.
Windows process table shows gateway-direct cloudflared PID 18368, bridge-facing cloudflared PID 3308, bridge node PID 11292, and OpenClaw-to-Codex watcher PID 18056.
Bridge rescue status reports TEMPORARY_GATEWAY_DIRECT plus BRIDGE_FACING route flags; publicMcpReady=false.

known_routes.route: redacted Cloudflare quick tunnel -> 127.0.0.1:8787 -> ChatGPT OpenClaw Bridge -> OpenClaw /hooks/agent -> codex-memory-operator
known_routes.risk: Proved for governance testing, not production ingress approval.

known_routes.route: ChatGPT MCP client -> TLS endpoint -> identity-aware ingress -> localhost/private bridge -> OpenClaw /hooks/agent -> target agent
known_routes.risk: Blocked until ingress, token_REDACTED_FIELD_NAME, persistence, pruning, and gateway exposure posture are resolved.

known_routes.route: OpenClaw transcript -> Watch-OpenClawToCodex.ps1 -> codex exec resume/new -> local Codex artifacts
known_routes.risk: Currently machine-level and headless; replies are artifact/log oriented, not a normal outbound chat channel.

short_human_summary: The ecosystem currently has one active primary agent, OpenClaw Main. Codex Memory Operator and Home Windows Codex are configured hook/bridge targets, but Codex Memory Operator is still experimental and Home Windows Codex was not observed as an active workspace. The active infrastructure includes OpenClaw gateway on 18789, the local bridge on 8787, a gateway-direct Cloudflare recovery tunnel, a bridge-facing test tunnel, and a watcher that dispatches OpenClaw WebChat messages into headless Codex runs. Public MCP is not ready: the gateway-direct public-by-URL route, bridge persistence, token_REDACTED_FIELD_NAME rotation finalization, idempotency ledger pruning, and production ingress policy remain blockers.
